source_paper_title: Splitting Guarantees for Prophet Inequalities via Nonlinear Systems
context:
Section 6 (Final Remarks), page 29 (PDF page 28/39 in the provided parsed text)

We leave the tightness of our approximation result in Theorem 2 through our nonlinear system (1)-(3) as an open question, i.e., whether \(\inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{n,k} = \sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell\).
title: Tightness of the nonlinear-system lower bound: is \(\inf_{n\ge 1}\gamma_{n,k}=\sum_{\ell=1}^k\theta^\star_\ell\)?
latex:
Let \(k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) and \(n\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge k}\). In the i.i.d. \(k\)-selection prophet inequality problem, there are \(n\) nonnegative i.i.d. random variables \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) with a known continuous distribution \(F\) supported on \(\mathbb{R}_+\). A (possibly randomized) online policy \(\pi\) observes \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) sequentially and must irrevocably decide, upon seeing \(X_t\), whether to accept it, subject to accepting at most \(k\) items in total. The policy’s payoff is the expected sum of accepted values.

Let \(X_{(1)}\le \cdots \le X_{(n)}\) denote the order statistics of \(X_1,\dots,X_n\). Define the offline benchmark
\[
\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{i=n-k+1}^n X_{(i)}\Big].
\]
Let \(V_{n,k}(\pi,F)\) be the expected payoff of \(\pi\) under \(F\). Define the optimal worst-case approximation ratio
\[
\gamma_{n,k}=\sup_{\pi}\;\inf_{F}\;\frac{V_{n,k}(\pi,F)}{\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)},
\]
where the infimum ranges over all continuous distributions \(F\) on \(\mathbb{R}_+\) for which \(\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)\in(0,\infty)\).

For each \(k\), consider the nonlinear system of differential equations on \([0,1)\) for unknown constants \(\theta_1,\dots,\theta_k\in\mathbb{R}_+\) and unknown functions \(y_1,\dots,y_k:(0,1)\to(0,1]\):
- For \(\ell\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) and \(x\ge 0\), let \(\Gamma_\ell(x)=\int_x^{\infty} t^{\ell-1}e^{-t}\,dt\) (upper incomplete gamma function).
- The system is
\[
\big(\Gamma_k(-\ln y_k(t))\big)'=k!\Big(1-\frac{1}{k\theta_k}\Big)-\Gamma_{k+1}(-\ln y_k(t)),
\]
\[
\big(\Gamma_k(-\ln y_j(t))\big)'=k!-\Gamma_{k+1}(-\ln y_j(t)) - \frac{\theta_{j+1}}{\theta_j}\Big(k!-\Gamma_{k+1}(-\ln y_{j+1}(t))\Big)\quad\text{for all }j\in\{1,\dots,k-1\},
\]
with boundary conditions
\[
y_j(0)=1\quad\text{and}\quad \lim_{t\uparrow 1} y_j(t)=0\quad\text{for all }j\in\{1,\dots,k\}.
\]
Assume \(\theta^\star_1,\dots,\theta^\star_k\) are constants for which this system has a solution.

Problem: determine whether the asymptotically best worst-case approximation ratio equals the sum of these constants, namely whether
\[
\inf_{n\ge k}\gamma_{n,k}=\sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell.
\]
Equivalently, is \(\sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell\) the exact worst-case approximation ratio (over all \(n\)) for the i.i.d. \(k\)-selection prophet inequality?

open_problem_quote: We leave the tightness of our approximation result in Theorem 2 through our nonlinear system (1)-(3) as an open question, i.e., whether \(\inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{n,k} = \sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell\).
